timeline.label: FY2025 OTC annual filed: >1.3M mineral acres, 36,705 leased acres, 1,126,284 shares outstanding
H1 2025 semi-annual: mineral lease income $189K vs $149K prior year; net loss ($51K)
FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn
Repurchased 51,633 shares at $27/sh; acquired 667,000 additional mineral acres (~$1.52/acre)
Timberland partial liquidation: ~$100/sh special distributions ($92 + $8) from timber asset sale; retained mineral business
Shareholder approval of timber sale and partial liquidation; company refocused on mineral leasing and royalties

industry.structure: KEWL is a pure mineral lessor on Michigan/Wisconsin acreage, not an operator. Revenue is lease rent; Copperwood royalties would accrue if Highland Copper reaches production. OTC Pink listing with thin liquidity and concentrated insider ownership (Mai ~29.6%, Speron ~11.6%).
industry.share_shift: After the 2021 timber liquidation the company shrank to a mineral-only estate. Highland Copper holds the dominant Copperwood lease; secondary lessees on the 667K-acre 2024 expansion remain the main incremental option.
industry.trend: Copper demand tailwind from electrification and data-center build-out supports option value, but spot price already embeds much of Copperwood upside at ~4.3× GAAP book. Michigan mining infrastructure grants could accelerate Highland Copper timing.
